preamble: The simplification of final stop consonants like [p] into a glottal stop [ʔ] is a core process in Tonogenesis, specifically in the development of 'Checked Tones' (Rù tone). This debuccalization preserves the abrupt syllable closure while losing the specific place of articulation.
phoneticEffects: Coda Reduction, Debuccalization, Tonogenesis, Checked Tone

examples.note: Simplification of final labial stops to a glottal coda in some dialects.
A stage in the development of the Rù (入) tone as syllables lost their original oral codas.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
